title: Program Manager / Gestionnaire de programme
description:
Vous souhaitez faire partie d’une organisation propulsée par sa mission qui façonne le futur de l’innovation au Canada? Vous aimez évoluer dans des milieux collaboratifs où la créativité, l’amélioration continue et la pensée stratégique sont valorisées? Êtes‑vous une personne motivée par des responsabilités qui contribuent à accroître la compétitivité du Canada et à produire des résultats concrets pour les communautés et les entreprises? 

Pour développer la capacité d’innovation du Canada, à Mitacs, nous misons sur le talent. Nous établissons des liens entre les chercheurs et chercheuses et les organisations dans toutes les régions du pays pour aider à résoudre des défis pressants et accélérer la croissance économique. En facilitant la collaboration entre le secteur privé et le milieu postsecondaire et en appuyant le perfectionnement de talents hautement qualifiés, nous contribuons à une économique canadienne résiliente et capable de relever les défis de demain.

Guidé·es par nos valeurs, impact, intégrité, collaboration, excellence et talent, nous collaborons avec des partenaires de partout au pays pour faire avancer la recherche, la commercialisation et l’innovation. 

Description du poste :
Nous sommes à la recherche d’un ou d’une gestionnaire de programme pour diriger la planification et la mise en œuvre de programmes du Bureau de gestion de la transformation, y compris, sans s’y limiter, la transformation organisationnelle de Mitacs (MET).

GET STARTED

CAREERS

For developers,
by developers.

Helping companies move fast by removing their bottlenecks.

ABOUT PINGRAM
CONTACT US
SEC. 01
OPEN ROLES
We're hiring

FREELANCE / PART-TIME

Sponsored OSS Developer

If you would like to write and release an SDK in a programming language or framework we don't yet support, we can sponsor your work.

Assuming you follow our SDK guidelines, we'll gladly pay you a market-competitive day rate for your development time.

APPLY NOW
SEC. 02
PHILOSOPHY
Built for perpetuity

Dev tools are different.

You can't "move fast and break things" if the dev tools you rely on do the same: raise prices as they experiment, shut down features when they pivot, or change org structure without warning.

So we decided to build the opposite:

A stable business without external funding
A strong focus on quality and affordability
Unit economics strong enough to do this forever

At our core, we are builders passionate about building a great product.

SEC. 03
VALUES
Product, people, perpetuity
01
Product

Build something you'd be proud of. Build it in the image of your beautiful mind.

02
People

The real treasure is the developers you meet along the way. Have some fun.

03
Perpetuity

Built to last. One day your grandchildren's notifications will go through here.

SEC. 04
CULTURE
Working at Pingram
01
Developers' Club

Except for legal and finance, everyone at Pingram has an extensive development background.

Create a Future with Us

College of the North Atlantic (CNA) is committed to providing an inclusive learning environment which celebrates and encourages diversity. CNA promotes equal access and opportunities to facilitate success in the educational community and in the community at large.

CNA strives to create an organizational culture that welcomes and amplifies diverse perspectives, nurtures belonging and actively seeks to eliminate barriers to equity. We invite all interested individuals to apply, and as part of our shared commitment to the advancement of equity, diversity and inclusion (EDI) as a true reflection of CNA’s core values (Excellence, Integrity, Diversity, Respect and Transparency), we encourage applications from equity-seeking groups including, but not limited to, Indigenous Peoples, racialized persons, persons with disabilities, those who identify as women, and members of 2SLGBTQIA+ communities.

There are currently no active job postings. We encourage you to visit our careers page regularly for other upcoming opportunities.

For further information about a career at the Office of the Auditor General, please contact our Manager of People and Culture, Andrea Milley at andreamilley@oag.nl.ca or call 709-725-3351.

The Office of the Auditor General values diversity in the workplace and is an equal-opportunity employer.

Disability-related accommodations and alternate formats related to competitions are available on request by contacting 709-725-3351.

Preference will be given to applicants who are Canadian citizens or permanent residents of Canada, or those who are legally entitled to work in Canada.

Competitions posted may be used to fill future similar vacancies with the Office of the Auditor General.

How to Apply:

Applications must be received on or before the closing date stated for a job posting. It is the responsibility of the applicant to submit an application that demonstrates the required merit criteria. All applications must contain accurate contact information, including current mailing address, email address and phone number. All information submitted as part of an application must be factual, complete and current to the date of submission. Applicants must submit by email to careers@oag.nl.ca

Applications should be consolidated into a single PDF document

Civilian Opportunities

The RNC has roughly 100 civilian employees who play an important role in the delivery of police services every day. Civilians are employed in a wide variety of disciplines including finance, administration, planning, communication, information management and technology, legal services, as well as asset and facilities management.

Civilian employment opportunities available within the RNC are advertised through the Public Service Commission, Government of Newfoundland and Labrador. Visit https://www.hiring.gov.nl.ca to view current job postings.

Work with NLPL
 

With a network of 94 sites, Newfoundland and Labrador Public Libraries provides library services throughout the province of Newfoundland and Labrador.  Embracing the diversity of our patrons and their cultures, we offer resources that include traditional library services as well as modern innovative programs, spaces, and technologies.

If you are a community-minded individual who enjoys helping people of all ages, we want you to join our team!

For other job postings within the Newfoundland & Labrador Public Service, please visit the Public Service Commission’s Career Centre.

title: Reporting & Analytics Business Process Owner (Two Year Term)
description: Venor is proud to partner with the Nova Scotia Liquor Corporation (NSLC) on their search for a Reporting & Analytics Business Process Owner to join their team in Halifax, Nova Scotia. The Nova Scotia Liquor Corporation (NSLC) plays an important role in the province, responsibly managing the sale of beverage alcohol and cannabis while investing back into the communities it serves. Through innovation, operational excellence, and a commitment to public service, the NSLC continues to modernize its business and technology landscape to better serve Nova Scotians. As part of this transformation, NSLC is embarking on a significant SAP modernization initiative and is seeking a Reporting & Analytics Business Process Owner (BPO) to help shape the future of enterprise reporting and analytics. This two-year term opportunity offers the chance to play a pivotal role in one of the organization's most strategic transformation programs, ensuring data, reporting, and business intelligence capabilities are designed to support informed decision-making across the enterprise. Reporting to the Director, Insights & Strategy, this role will serve as the primary business owner for reporting strategy, analytics alignment, and enterprise data definitions while partnering closely with business leaders, project teams, and technology stakeholders throughout the SAP implementation. The Opportunity This role sits at the intersection of business strategy, analytics, and enterprise transformation. Acting as the

title: Senior Structural Engineer
location: St John's, Nova Scotia, New Brunswick, Newfoundland & Labrador, Ontario, CA
description: Senior Structural Engineer  Venor is proud to partner with Tiller Engineering Inc. in their search for a Senior Structural Engineer.  At Tiller Engineering, you'll join a team that has built its reputation on delivering practical, innovative structural engineering solutions for over two decades. Since 2001, Tiller has partnered with clients across the telecommunications, infrastructure, and energy sectors, providing engineering expertise on projects that strengthen communities throughout Atlantic Canada.  As the company continues to grow, they are looking for an experienced Structural Engineer who is eager to take on technically challenging work, mentor others, and play an influential role in shaping projects from concept through completion.  The Opportunity  This is an opportunity to step into a senior technical role where your expertise will directly influence the success of a wide variety of projects. You'll lead structural analysis and design, provide technical oversight, collaborate with multidisciplinary teams, and build trusted relationships with clients and project partners.  You'll enjoy the autonomy to make sound engineering decisions while working alongside experienced professionals who value collaboration, practical thinking, and delivering quality work. Whether you're designing new infrastructure, assessing existing structures, or solving complex engineering challenges, you'll have the opportunity to make a meaningful impact every day.  What You'll Do  As a Senio

title: Dental Faculty Coordinator
description: Venor is proud to partner with Keyin College in the search for a Dental Faculty Coordinator for its Level II Dental Assisting Program.  About Keyin College  Keyin College is one of Newfoundland and Labrador's leading post-secondary institutions, with more than 40,000 graduates since 1980. As the province's largest private college, Keyin delivers career-focused education that equips learners with the practical, in-demand skills needed to succeed in today's workforce.  The Level II Dental Assisting Program reflects this commitment through a nationally approved curriculum that combines classroom learning with hands-on clinical experience in fully equipped dental clinics. This immersive approach equips students with the knowledge, confidence, and real-world skills required to thrive in modern dental practices.  Guided by a collaborative, student-centred culture, Keyin College is committed to educational excellence while fostering the success of its students, faculty, and staff. Joining Keyin means becoming part of an institution dedicated to developing the next generation of skilled healthcare professionals.  The Role  Keyin College is looking for a Dental Faculty Coordinator to lead and support the Level II Dental Assisting Program. This role is responsible for overseeing program delivery, fostering instructional excellence, and ensuring the curriculum continues to meet professional, regulatory, and industry standards. Working closely with instructors, students, and campus leade

title: Senior Sales Engineer
description: Venor is proud to partner with InnovMarine on their search for a Senior Sales Engineer to join their growing team! InnovMarine delivers innovative software solutions and consulting services that help organizations optimize complex asset management operations across the maritime, defence, and industrial sectors. Combining deep technical expertise with industry knowledge, InnovMarine partners with organizations to modernize operations, improve decision-making, and unlock greater value through digital transformation and emerging technologies. As InnovMarine continues to expand across Atlantic Canada, they are seeking a Senior Sales Engineer to help grow their presence within the maritime and defence industries. This unique opportunity blends technical pre-sales expertise with consultative enterprise sales, making it ideal for someone who enjoys translating complex business challenges into practical technology solutions. Reporting directly to the President, you'll play a key role in expanding existing enterprise relationships while developing new business opportunities throughout Atlantic Canada. Working closely with technical and consulting teams, you'll help organizations leverage solutions such as QuickBrain and IBM Maximo to improve asset management, operational performance, and long-term business outcomes. This is a remote position open to candidates residing in Atlantic Canada, with regular travel throughout the region. The Opportunity This role sits at the intersection of

title: Accountant
description: Venor is proud to partner with Archibald Drilling & Blasting in their search for an Accountant to join their team in Truro, Nova Scotia. Archibald Drilling & Blasting is a leading provider of drilling and blasting services, with more than 40 years of experience delivering safe, innovative, and reliable solutions across quarry operations, open-pit mining, civil construction, and marine projects. Known for its commitment to safety, technical excellence, and continuous improvement, Archibald supports some of the most complex and demanding projects throughout Atlantic Canada and beyond. Reporting directly to General Manager, the Accountant will play a critical role in the day-to-day financial operations of the business. This is a highly visible and well-rounded position that will touch all aspects of accounting and finance, while helping leadership navigate cash flow management, operational reporting, and continuous process improvement. The successful candidate will thrive in a fast-paced environment, bring a proactive and solutions-focused mindset, and be motivated by the opportunity to help modernize and improve financial processes as the business continues to grow. What you’ll be doing Managing full-cycle accounting functions across the organization Overseeing accounts payable, accounts receivable, payroll support, reconciliations, journal entries, and general ledger maintenance. title: Director of Operations
description: Venor is proud to partner with Laura Gatien & Associates in their search for a Director of Operations to join their team. They are seeking a people-centric leader who can bring structure, clarity, and steady leadership while championing the values that make LGA unique. About Laura Gatien & Associates Laura Gatien & Associates is a growing counselling practice dedicated to providing accessible, client-focused mental health support within the community. Guided by compassion, professionalism, and a deep commitment to social impact, LGA supports individuals and families through evidence-based care and a welcoming environment. Their work is shaped by five core values:  Warmth, Dedication, Vulnerability, Equality, and Growth, which inform how they care for clients, collaborate as a team, and show up for their community. This role is a strong fit for an experienced operations leader who enjoys working alongside a dynamic entrepreneur to bring ideas to life and turn vision into execution. The Director of Operations will serve as the CEO’s essential complement, harmonizing the major functions of the business, fostering accountability, strengthening culture, and driving sustainable traction. What Success Looks Like This is not a role where you'll be expected to take the reins on day one. Instead, you'll benefit from a thoughtful, phased onboarding designed to set you up for long-term success. title: Senior Platform Engineer
description: Venor is proud to partner with QuickFacts on their search for a Senior Platform Engineer to join their growing team! QuickFacts is a Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) company transforming how insurance professionals access and manage information. Their platform brings together underwriting guidelines, carrier documentation, workflows, training resources, and comparison tools into a single, searchable experience that saves clients valuable time and improves decision-making. As QuickFacts continues to expand its AI-powered platform, they are seeking a seasoned backend engineer who thrives on building scalable systems, solving complex technical challenges, and developing innovative solutions that deliver meaningful business impact. This is an opportunity to join a growing technology company where your work will directly influence product strategy, platform architecture, and the future of AI-driven information management within the insurance industry. The Opportunity As a Senior Platform Engineer, you will play a critical role in designing and scaling the services, infrastructure, APIs, and data systems that power QuickFacts' next generation of products. This position sits at the intersection of software engineering, large-scale information systems, and applied AI. You'll work closely with Product, Front-End Engineering, and DevOps teams to transform complex insurance data into intelligent customer experiences. title: Senior Project Manager
description: Our client, Nexco Construction Company is growing and seeking an ambitious and results-driven Senior Project Manager based in Dartmouth, Nova Scotia. This is an opportunity for a proven construction leader who thrives on ownership, enjoys solving complex challenges, and wants to play a key role in delivering impactful civil infrastructure projects.  About the Role  As a Senior Project Manager, you will lead projects from pre-construction through closeout, overseeing all aspects of planning, execution, financial performance, and client management. Working closely with operations, field leadership, subcontractors, engineers, and clients, you will be empowered to drive project success while contributing to the continued growth and evolution of the organization. We are looking for a leader who is proactive, highly accountable, and motivated by delivering exceptional results in a fast-paced, entrepreneurial environment.  Key Responsibilities  Lead the successful delivery of civil construction projects from pre-construction through completion and closeout.   Develop, manage, and maintain project schedules, budgets, forecasts, and resource plans.   Oversee project financial performance, identifying risks and opportunities to maximize profitability.   Coordinate and manage subcontractors, suppliers, consultants, and internal project teams.   Ensure projects are executed safely, efficiently, and in accordance with contract requirements and quality standards.   Build and maintain stron

title: Social Worker
description: Venor is proud to partner with Oromocto First Nation Child and Family Services in their search for a Social Worker to join their team. Oromocto First Nation Child and Family Services (OCFS) is a designated child and family services agency dedicated to serving the children and families of Oromocto First Nation. Grounded in community, culture, and collaboration, OCFS works to keep children safe, strengthen families, and ensure children remain connected to their identity, traditions, and support systems. Unlike larger agencies, OCFS serves a single community, allowing for smaller caseloads and a more personalized approach to care. Team members work closely together, creating an environment where social workers can devote meaningful time and attention to each child and family while receiving strong organizational support. Reporting to the Director of OCFS, the Social Worker will play a critical role in promoting the safety, wellbeing, and long-term success of children and families within the community. This is a full-time, on-site opportunity based in Oromocto First Nation, New Brunswick, with local travel required and participation in a rotating after-hours on-call schedule. What you'll be doing Conduct child protection investigations, risk assessments, and safety assessments to support the wellbeing of children and families. title: Branch Manager
description: Our client, Graybar is seeking a dynamic and results-driven Branch Manager to lead and grow the branch operations in Kentville, Nova Scotia. This role is ideal for a strategic leader who thrives on developing business opportunities, driving operational excellence, and building high-performing teams. Reporting to the Regional Vice President, the Branch Manager, you will have full accountability for branch performance—balancing sales growth, customer satisfaction, financial management, and employee development—while acting as a key ambassador of the company in the local market. What You’ll Do Leadership & Strategy Provide strategic direction and lead the execution of branch initiatives aligned with company objectives. Champion a culture of accountability, integrity, collaboration, and continuous improvement. Act as a role model by consistently following company policies, procedures, and values. Sales & Customer Growth Maintain and grow sales volume while strengthening customer relationships. Identify and develop new business opportunities, products, and customers. Build strong relationships with customers and suppliers through meetings, calls, and industry events. Ensure all reasonable customer requests are handled promptly and professionally. Operations & Financial Management Oversee pricing strategies, inventory management, and inventory turns. Manage accounts receivable, credit risks, and cost controls. title: Manager of Corporate Accounting
description: Venor is proud to partner with Graybar Canada on their search for a Manager of Corporate Accounting to join their head office team in Halifax, NS. Graybar Canada is a leader in the distribution of high-quality electrical, automation, and telecommunications products, supporting these operations through supply chain management and logistics services. Graybar Canada operates more than 35 branches across Canada. As one of Canada's largest employee-owned companies, Graybar Canada has the power and stability of a big corporation and the integrity and drive of a neighborhood business. What You’ll Be Doing The Manager of Corporate Accounting will support the Director of Accounting and VP, Finance in managing the day-to-day operations of the finance function for the organization. This is a key leadership role within the finance team, ensuring financial statements are accurate, compliant with accounting standards, and delivered on time. We're looking for a collaborative, hands-on professional who enjoys being involved in the details, isn't afraid to roll up their sleeves, and is eager to learn. Success in this role will come from a combination of technical capability, initiative, adaptability, and a genuine desire to contribute and gain new skills. Key Responsibilities Financial Reporting & Close: Support the complete accounting cycle: journal entries, accruals, prepayments, depreciation, account reconciliations & compiling working papers Support the Controller with monthly, quarterly,

title: Office Administrator & Bookkeeper
description: Venor is proud to partner with Archibald Drilling & Blasting in their search for an Office Administrator & Bookkeeper to join their team in Truro, Nova Scotia. Archibald Drilling & Blasting is a leading provider of drilling and blasting services, with more than 40 years of experience delivering safe, innovative, and reliable solutions across quarry operations, open-pit mining, civil construction, and marine projects. Known for its commitment to safety, technical excellence, and continuous improvement, Archibald supports some of the most complex and demanding projects throughout Atlantic Canada and beyond. As the company continues to grow, Archibald is seeking an organized, detail-oriented, and proactive Office Administrator & Bookkeeper to support day-to-day office operations and financial administration. This is a critical role within the organization, ideal for someone who enjoys variety in their work and thrives in a small business environment where they can make a meaningful impact. Reporting directly to leadership, the successful candidate will oversee office administration while managing core accounting and bookkeeping functions. This position offers the opportunity to help shape and improve internal processes as the company continues to evolve. title: Labour Relations Specialist
description: Venor is proud to partner with J.D. Irving, Limited in their search for a new Labour Relations Specialist to join their Human Resources team. J.D. Irving, Limited (JDI) is a premier North American forestry and operations leader. What began in 1882 as a regional, family-owned enterprise has grown into a dynamic, coast-to-coast group of operating divisions with over 140 years of success. Recognized for building a highly efficient, vertically integrated supply chain, J.D. Irving, Limited delivers high-quality lumber, pulp and paper products, and sustainable forest management across Eastern Canada and the United States. At Grand Lake Timber Sawmill, Chipman, NB, JDI is proud of their strong legacy in sustainable forestry and high-quality wood products. Reporting to the HR Manager, the Labour Relations Specialist will play a key role in supporting positive union-management relations, ensuring compliance with collective agreements, and promoting a culture of fairness and accountability. They will support the business by interpreting and administering the collective agreement, maintaining a productive relationship with the Union, managing the grievance process, providing training and development to supervisors on company policies and labour relations best practices, assisting with progressive discipline practices, resolving complex labour relations issues and conducting workplace investigations in conjunction with Industrial Security Ltd. title: EDI and API Integration Analyst
description: Venor is proud to partner with Seaboard Armour Transportation and Logistics in their search for an EDI and API Integration Analyst to join their team. Seaboard Armour Transportation and Logistics is a premier North American transportation and logistics leader. What began in 1964 as a regional, family-owned petroleum delivery operation in Cape Breton, Nova Scotia, has grown into a coast-to-coast group of companies with over 60 years of success. Recognized as one of Canada’s Best Managed Companies and an industry Employer of Choice, Seaboard Armour delivers safe, reliable, and innovative bulk transport, freight forwarding, and supply chain solutions across Canada and the United States. As part of their growing Data and Integration team,  the EDI and API Integration Analyst is responsible for the operation, support, documentation, monitoring, onboarding, and continuous improvement of customer and partner data integrations across EDI, API, and file-based exchange methods. This role ensures integration processes are reliable, well-documented, supportable, and aligned to business needs across transportation and logistics operations. As valued members of the expanding Data and Integration team, EDI and API Integration Analysts, along with current team of data integration professionals, collectively manage, support, document, monitor, onboard, and continuously enhance customer and partner data integrations across EDI, API, and file-based exchange methods. title: Power BI Report Developer
description: Venor is proud to partner with Seaboard Armour Transportation and Logistics in their search for a Power BI Report Developer to join their team. Seaboard Armour Transportation and Logistics is a premier North American transportation and logistics leader. What began in 1964 as a regional, family-owned petroleum delivery operation in Cape Breton, Nova Scotia, has grown into a coast-to-coast group of companies with over 60 years of success. Recognized as one of Canada’s Best Managed Companies and an industry Employer of Choice, Seaboard Armour delivers safe, reliable, and innovative bulk transport, freight forwarding, and supply chain solutions across Canada and the United States. As part of their growing Data and Integration team, Seaboard Armour Transportation and Logistics is seeking a Power BI Report Developer to design, develop, validate, and support trusted Power BI reports, dashboards, datasets, and analytics solutions across transportation, logistics, dispatch, billing, finance, safety, maintenance, customer service, and executive performance management. With a strong focus on report quality, performance, documentation, user acceptance testing, and adoption of standardized reporting practices, this role helps improve operational visibility, reduce manual reporting effort, and build a more mature analytics foundation for the business. title: Marketing Coordinator
description: Venor is proud to partner with Fidelis Law in their search for a Marketing Coordinator to join their team in Moncton, NB Fidelis Law is a dedicated personal injury firm with offices in Moncton and Edmundston, New Brunswick. The firm is committed to providing exceptional legal representation to personal injury victims while delivering personalized, compassionate service that ensures clients’ rights are protected and their voices are heard. As Fidelis Law continues to grow, they are seeking a Marketing Coordinator to support and execute marketing initiatives that strengthen brand awareness, enhance community presence, and support business development objectives. Reporting to the Partners of the firm, the Marketing Coordinator will play a key role in coordinating campaigns, creating engaging content, and supporting the day-to-day marketing activities that promote the firm's services and values. This is an exciting opportunity for a creative and organized marketing professional who enjoys working in a collaborative environment and contributing to meaningful work that positively impacts clients and communities across New Brunswick. What you'll be doing: Support the planning, coordination, and execution of marketing campaigns across digital, print, social media, and community engagement channels. title: Vice President of Finance
description: Venor is excited to partner with the Nexco Group on their search for a Vice President of Finance to join their team based in Dartmouth, NS. Nexco Group is a proudly Nova Scotian, integrated group of companies delivering trusted solutions across civil construction, traffic control, and infrastructure services. Through its divisions, Nexco Construction, Nexco Traffic Control, and Nexco Environmental, the company plays a vital role in building and maintaining the infrastructure that supports communities across Atlantic Canada. With expertise spanning mass excavation, site development, demolition, road building, highway structures and bridges, retaining walls, and traffic management, Nexco Group is known for its commitment to safety, quality, reliability, and collaboration. Driven by a passion for excellence and a people-first approach, Nexco partners with clients to successfully deliver projects that contribute to Atlantic Canada's continued growth and development. About the Role: We are seeking a highly strategic Vice President of Finance to provide executive-level oversight for the Nexco Group. As the ultimate owner of our financial vision, you will drive big-picture planning, manage complex cash flow and risk, and optimize internal controls to ensure sustainable, profitable growth across Nexco and a subsidiary of companies. Key Responsibilities: Strategic Financial Leadership & Planning Serve as the primary financial visionary for the Nexco Group, aligning financial strategie

title: Supply Chain Manager
description: SureShot Solutions designs, manufactures, and markets the most innovative dispensers of beverages and beverage add-ins, quenching the thirst of anyone looking for a better way to serve refreshments. With many industry firsts, they are passionate about identifying customer needs, and are tenacious about providing breakthrough solutions to address them. Over the years, they have proudly collaborated with top quick-serve restaurant chains and convenience retailers worldwide to design, engineer, manufacture, and market dispensers for beverages and beverage enhancers. At the heart of their success lies a deep-rooted commitment to excellence, where innovation meets unparalleled craftsmanship. Adaptability to change and a commitment to ensuring a safe workplace while delivering top-notch quality results are qualities SureShot holds in high regard. On behalf of SureShot, we are recruiting a Supply Chain Manager to lead key functional areas including procurement, inventory performance management, planning, and scheduling, while providing direct leadership to the Logistics Lead & Scheduler and the Purchasing Agent. Are you a strategic and systems-savvy supply chain professional who thrives on optimizing operations, leading teams, and delivering results? Reporting directly to the Vice President of Finance and Administration, this role is a critical member of the Supply Chain Management (SCM) Team and plays a central role in driving operational efficiency and supplier performance. title: Bilingual Senior Manager, Operations – New Brunswick
description: Venor is proud to partner with Circular Materials in their search for a Bilingual Senior Manager, Operations – New Brunswick to join their team. Circular Materials is a national producer-led not for profit organization that supports producers in meeting their obligations under extended producer responsibility regulations across Canada. They are committed to building efficient and effective recycling systems to minimize waste and ensure materials are reused again and again. Through the support of their founders and member producers, Circular Materials is implementing a national harmonized approach focused on improving recovery rates, meeting the needs and expectations of consumers, and protecting the natural environment. Reporting to the Managing Director, Atlantic, the Senior Manager, Operations – NB will play a pivotal leadership role in overseeing New Brunswick’s recycling common collection and post-collection systems under Provincial EPR legislation. This is a unique opportunity to shape the future of recycling in Atlantic Canada, build meaningful stakeholder relationships, and lead a team driving real environmental impact. What You’ll Be Doing Collection & Post-Collection Network Development Develop subject matter expertise on the regulatory requirements associated with the Stewardship Plan and the NB Designated Materials Packaging and Paper Regulations. Manage the PPP recycling common collection system and post-collection system in NB, in alignment with the program plan

title: Controller

Nexco Group is a proudly Nova Scotian, integrated group of companies delivering trusted solutions across civil construction, traffic control, and infrastructure services. Driven by a passion for excellence and a people-first approach, Nexco partners with clients to successfully deliver projects that contribute to Nova Scotia's continued growth and development. Reporting directly to the VP of Finance, the Controller will play a critical role in overseeing the day-to-day accounting operations of the organization while providing financial insight to support strategic decision-making. Description

Hospitality is in your blood! 

A runner is the dynamic link that keeps the team moving forward seamlessly. Whether it's delivering crucial items or swiftly executing tasks, their speed and efficiency are essential for the team's success.

Please note that this is a part time position on evenings + weekends 

 

WHAT YOU'LL DO

Communicate effectively with staff to coordinate the timely preparation and delivery of requests.
Assist in maintaining cleanliness and organization, including clearing and resetting tables as needed.
Collaborate with servers to address any guest requests or concerns promptly and professionally.

Amal is an innovative and vibrant organization providing a diverse range of programs and services. We pride ourselves on our creative approaches to meeting the needs of young people, families and communities, and work hard to provide responsive, respectful and effective support and intervention.

We employ individuals in many disciplines, including social work, accounting, therapy, office administration and human resources, with our largest employee group consisting of child and youth care workers. Our workforce is regularly expanding as our organization grows.

If you are enthusiastic, passionate, committed to excellence and willing to work in a Growth Culture, then Amal may be the place for you.

Send your resume and cover letter to careers@amalnl.ca 

ABOUT

The vision of Amal Youth and Family Centre is to become a centre of hope, and a warm, welcoming, respectful and diverse organization in which children, youth and families from myriad backgrounds thrive and succeed together.

title: Associate/ Senior Associate, Transaction Services
description: Putting people first, every day BDO is a firm built on a foundation of positive relationships with our people and our clients. Each day, our professionals provide exceptional service, helping clients with advice and insight they can trust. In turn, we offer an award-winning environment that fosters a people-first culture with a high priority on your personal and professional growth. Your Opportunity Our Toronto office is looking for an Associate/ Senior Associate, Transaction Services to join the Deal Advisory team. This is a new role, and the successful candidate would own the following responsibilities: Perform market, industry and company research; Evaluate a business’ historical performance and financial projections; Understand key accounting policies and management control environments; Perform various in-depth financial analyses on companies including analyzing and understanding the sustainability of earnings and cash flows, operating working capital requirements, quality of assets and key drivers of financial performance (revenues and margins, operating expenses, etc.); Interpret analysis and assist in the preparation of detailed buy-side and sell-side due diligence reports; Undertake smaller assignments or assist on larger assignments reporting to a manager, senior manager, or a partner; Support various business development initiatives including pitches and proposals; Communicate directly with various transaction counterparties including the client, target, potential

JUNIOR MECHANICAL ENGINEER - C-CORE

At C-CORE, we are committed to investing in people and creating a dynamic and rewarding employment experience. We are seeking a Junior Mechanical Engineer to join our Oceans & Energy Team. This position is based in St. John’s, NL.

If you have a passion for complex design solutions, hands-on fabrication, and contributing to cutting-edge R&D projects from concept to commissioning, this opportunity may be for you. Are you enthusiastic about engineering, attentive to detail, and motivated to support a diverse team of professionals in a dynamic environment? If so, we want you on our team!

About C-CORE

C-CORE conducts R&D and provides research-based advisory services, delivering innovative engineering and technology solutions to national and international clients. For over 50 years, we have built a world-class team of professionals in Remote Sensing and Oceans & Energy. We are global leaders in Earth Observation and geospatial intelligence, with our flagship platform Coresight driving major contracts in defense, environmental monitoring, and advanced R&D.

Position Overview

As a Junior Mechanical Engineer, you will be responsible for contributing to and completing complex design solutions by applying principles of mechanical, structural, hydraulic, and electrical engineering. You will operate within a collaborative team environme

About Wedgwood Insurance

At Wedgwood Insurance, our core values of We Care, We Lead, We Improve, and We Deliver are reflected in everything we do. It’s how we’ve become the most trusted insurance broker in Newfoundland & Labrador and have been part of our foundation for over 45 years. We invest in our team by promoting personal and professional development, providing amazing benefits, and fostering a culture of respect and pride in our work. And it shows as we’ve been named one of the Best Places to Work in Atlantic Canada by Atlantic Business Magazine!

We are looking for a System Specialist to join our St. John’s office.

Our Career Opportunity – System Specialist

This isn’t a support desk role. As Systems Specialist, you are the person who keeps the engine running. You’ll own the operation, maintenance, and optimization of our brokerage management system – primarily Applied EPIC – along with the supporting platforms our team depends on every day.

Cox & Palmer is a full-service, top-ranked Atlantic Canadian law firm with the knowledge and experience clients rely upon for solid legal solutions. The firm prides itself on a collegial work atmosphere and supportive team environment.

We hire self-motivated, team-minded individuals with a strong work ethic, integrity and a commitment to client service.

Managing, ordering, and stocking of all office supplies;
Basic maintenance of photocopiers and the postage machine;
Light tidying of pantries and the staff kitchen;
Providing reception backup relief as needed;

Assisting with special projects and/or administrative duties assigned by the office manager; and
Depositing outgoing mail at end of day.

Spoken and written proficiency in English is required; bilingualism is considered an asset;
1 year of experience working in an administrative role or professional services environment;
Must have their own vehicle for daily banking errands as needed (mileage is paid);

CO-OP ENGINEERING

Are you ready to apply your engineering knowledge in a real-world environment and gain hands-on experience supporting the electrical distribution system that powers communities across Nova Scotia?

We’re looking for an Electrical Engineering Co-op Student to join our Regional Engineering team in Sackville for the Fall term. This is an exciting opportunity to work alongside experienced engineers and technical professionals, contributing to projects that support the safe, reliable, and efficient operation of our distribution network.

As a member of our team, you’ll gain practical engineering experience, develop your technical skills, and build a deeper understanding of utility operations while making meaningful contributions to ongoing projects and initiatives.

Support the operation, planning, and design of the electrical distribution network.
Review customer servicing submissions and fault-level requests.
Update underground drawing records and support markup of new revisions.
Gather and analyze field data related to distribution assets.
Develop an understanding of electrical overcurrent and protective devices used on the distribution network.
Collaborate with engineers, technical staff, and operational teams on a variety of projects and initiatives.

PROJECT CONTROLS COORDINATOR (PCO)

Position Description:

The ideal candidate is highly organized, analytical, and detail-oriented, with strong financial tracking, reporting, and stakeholder coordination skills. Experience with Clarity, SharePoint, Confluence, and Jira is considered an asset.

Coordinate project activities across multiple initiatives.
Support both Project Manager-led and non-Project Manager-managed projects.
Track project budgets, spending, forecasts, and financials.

Prepare project status, financial, and risk reports.

About CoLab

At CoLab, we help mechanical engineering teams bring life-changing products to market years sooner.

CoLab is the AI platform for driving stronger engineering decisions. Every design review in CoLab builds a knowledge repository of design feedback, decisions, and lessons learned - which AI agents draw from to flag issues on future designs before they compound. The more your team works in CoLab, the smarter it gets and the faster you arrive at the ideal design. Companies like Ford, Komatsu, and Johnson Controls use CoLab to catch issues earlier, eliminate rework cycles, and bring products to market faster.

Founded in St. John’s, Newfoundland, CoLab has grown quickly from our first customer in 2019 to a rapidly scaling company. We’ve recently been recognized on Deloitte’s Fast 50™ and Fast 500™, and named Canadian company to watch by The Globe and Mail and Financial Post. 

About the Role: 

Mechanical engineering teams rely on a complex ecosystem of CAD, PLM, and enterprise systems to design, review, and manufacture products. At CoLab, we're building software that fits naturally into those workflows.

As a Senior Full Stack Developer, you'll play a key role in making that possible. You'll build the integrations and platform capabilities that connect CoLab with the systems our customers use every day, helping engineering organizations review designs faster, collaborate more effectively, and make better decision

About Us
At CSDS, we prioritize people—both within our team and in the communities we serve. Our mission is to redefine how Nova Scotians engage with government by creating accessible, reliable digital solutions centered around user needs.

In today’s tech-driven world, CSDS empowers government departments with essential digital solutions to meet Nova Scotians’ expectations. As a trusted partner across government, we enable departments to fulfill their mandates with secure, user-friendly services. From healthcare and education to public safety, we collaborate to transform how services are designed and delivered.

We don’t just implement technology; we modernize outdated systems and reimagine processes. Our agile, data-driven approach ensures that digital services are both efficient and adaptable to Nova Scotia’s evolving needs.

Joining CSDS means contributing to work that makes a real difference. From providing vital tech support to healthcare professionals to strengthening cybersecurity and developing scalable digital platforms, you’ll play a key role in building a more inclusive, responsive government for Nova Scotians.

Together, we’re setting new standards for digital public service, delivering resilient solutions that meet today’s needs—and anticipate tomorrow’s

Dreaming, designing, and delivering isn’t just a mantra for us — it’s a way of life. We believe that technology is the conduit, but our people – they are the connection that truly makes the magic happen!

Our Culture
We are a human-centered culture where we prioritize your well-being and are invested in helping you reach your fullest potential. We’re not only focused on achieving our goals — we’re dedicated to helping you achieve yours.

If you’re relentlessly curious, driven to make a difference, and collaborative at the core, then you belong with us.

 Job Posts Database Reliability Engineer

DATABASE RELIABILITY ENGINEER

Database Reliability Engineer
Halifax (Hybrid-2 days in office)
Our client a dynamic Information Technology services company that partners with leading global organizations to deliver innovative, high-quality IT solutions is looking for a Database Reliability Engineer.

They are looking for a motivated Database Reliability Engineer (DBRE) to join their expanding IT Operations team. The successful candidate will be part of a large global multi-international organization working with MySQL and PostgreSQL database platforms. You will own the end-to-end lifecycle of MySQL and PostgreSQL instances.

Responsibilities

Lifecycle Management​: Own the full lifecycle of MySQL and PostgreSQL instances, including provisioning, configuration, upgrades, monitoring, performance tuning, and decommissioning.
Provisioning & Deployment​: Use Infrastructure-as-Code tools (Terraform, Ansible, Puppet) to provision and manage MySQL and PostgreSQL clusters in a mixture of containerized (Docker/Kubernetes) and non-containerized environments.
Monitoring & Observability​: Implement and maintain monitoring dashboards, alerting, and health checks for database clusters to enable proactive incident detection and resolution.

Data Analyst
St. John's, Newfoundland and Labrador, Canada
Apply for this job

 

 Data Analyst

 

Newfoundland and Labrador Medical Association (NLMA)

 

ethree Consulting is proud to partner with the Newfoundland and Labrador Medical Association in their search for a Data Analyst with The Family Practice Renewal Program (FPRP), a joint initiative of the NLMA and the Department of Health and Community Services (DHCS). The NLMA functions as the employer for staff of FPRP.

 

Who We Are

The Newfoundland and Labrador Medical Association (NLMA) is the united voice of physicians across the province. As a member-driven, non-profit organization, we advance the political, clinical, and economic interests of the medical profession while championing a fair, accessible, and equitable health care system for all residents.

 

We believe that strong advocacy, strategic action, and a deep commitment to equity not only improve the working lives of physicians, but they also directly enhance patient care and health outcomes across the province. Through advocacy, collaboration, and leadership, we help shape the future of health care in Newfoundland and Labrador, ensuring the system evolves to meet the needs of patients, providers, and communities.

 

Our mission drives us to innovate with those we serve and to build a health system that is accessible, sustainable, and trusted by all Newfoundlande

title: FastTrackHER Pilot Participant
description:
Skip to content
EMPLOYER SERVICES
CAREER & YOUTH SERVICES
ABOUT
NEWS & EVENTS
CONTACT
CAREER AND YOUTH SERVICES

Equiforce provides innovative, responsive, and industry-informed workforce development initiatives that build equitable pathways into the industries shaping Newfoundland and Labrador’s future and connect women and gender-diverse individuals with opportunity.

CAREER PROGRAMS
FASTTRACKHER

An innovative workforce readiness initiative aimed at preparing women for high-demand industries. FastTrackHER is a new pilot in development for 2026. Short, intensive, and industry-informed, it creates pathways into underrepresented fields through sector connections, relevant certifications, and direct insight into emerging industry opportunities. The first pilot focuses on Newfoundland and Labrador’s construction sector in the Avalon region, with future iterations expanding to other industries across the province.

PATHWAYS TO PLACE

A province-wide pilot launching in 2026 that connects newcomer and immigrant women with eight-week, paid, work-integrated learning placements in local organizations. Participants gain workplace readiness skills, valuable Canadian experience, professional networks, and wraparound supports that promote success and belonging. Employers, in turn, strengthen their capacity through structured guidance that supports inclusive hiring, onboarding, and workplace practices.

For more information and to learn how to participate, click here.  

CAREER PATHWAYS

This is a full-time position with no fixed end date, subject to external funding.

ACENET is seeking a Research Consultant in Quantum Computing to assist academic and industry clients in understanding, evaluating, and applying emerging quantum computing technologies alongside advanced computing resources (HPC, AI, Cloud) available through ACENET and the Digital Research Alliance of Canada.

This role is focused on enabling early exploration and responsible adoption of quantum computing, rather than hardware development or theoretical research. The successful candidate will have access to quantum computing platforms to support hands-on experimentation and applied projects.

Our Approach

Stewart McKelvey has a long history of being a leader in Atlantic Canada. And being a leader means embracing innovation.

We changed the traditional approach to law student recruitment in Atlantic Canada. Typically, Articled Clerks wait for “hire-back” decisions well into their articling year.

Stewart McKelvey is thinking differently.

We believe that success in the practice of law requires making a commitment for more than one summer or one year. Our innovative approach removes the traditional “hire-back” decision-making process, typically done at the end of the articling year. A 2L Summer Student will advance to becoming an Articled Clerk, then directly to an Associate at Stewart McKelvey.

Control Room Operator
Control Room Operator

NSB Omega is searching for a Control Room Operator for our MAJOR Oil & Gas Client in Grande Prairie, AB

Assignment Description

This is a contract assignment located in our Grande Prairie office, working a shift of 4 days on, 3 day off, 10 hour per day.


Responsibilities:

 

·       Monitor, analyze, and optimize oil and gas well performance using real-time surveillance systems, production data, and advanced optimization tools. 

 

·       Support and execute artificial lift optimization strategies, including setpoint adjustments, cycle-time optimization, performance analysis, and troubleshooting of plunger lift, rod pump, and gas lift systems. 

 

·       Identify, evaluate, and prioritize artificial lift candidates to maximize production, improve operating efficiency, and enhance well economics. 

 

·       Collaborate with Production Engineers, Field Operations, Maintenance personnel, and Field Optimization teams to develop and implement production enhancement opportunities and artificial lift solutions. 

 

·       Foster strong working relationships with field personnel and engineering teams to identify, investigate, and resolve operational, maintenance, and production performance issues.

Payables Coordinator II
Payables Coordinator II

Responsibilities:

Prepare cash disbursement vouchers/wire transfer requests with supporting documentation and route to appropriate parties for goods/services received, rate verification and DOAG approval.

Send cash disbursement voucher/bank instructions to local payment center for payment.

Coding of invoices according to Chart of Accounts and payables guidelines.

Tracking of contract expenditures and compliance with contract terms for non-drilling contracts.

Track Spent vs. contract value and requisition amount for planning and budgeting purpose and report any deviations to BSM.

Updating and maintaining received invoice log and processed invoice log.

Maintain filing system for invoices and supporting documents up-to-date.

Assist in the Drilling Controls Analyst in processing drilling-related invoices and other tasks as required.

Requirements:

B.S. Degree in Accounting, Finance or related fields

3-5 years of closely related experience

Excellent command of the English language is required.

Must have good analytical, communication and interpersonal skills.

Must be proficient in current communication mediums and technologies (i.e., Microsoft Office, telecommunications, email, meetings)

Knowledge of the payables process and accounting systems preferred.

Jobs
Instrument & Controls Analyst
Instrument & Controls Analyst

Will support validation and data quality efforts for Safety Instrumented Systems (SIS) and control systems within a large, regulated industrial environment. The primary focus of this role is Digital Cause & Effect (DiCE) automated metrics table population, Cause & Effect (C&E) document tag comparison, and control system tag validation. Must have experience with control systems, SIS (safety instrumented systems), cause & effect testing, documentation and validation.

Responsibilities:
• Populate and maintain automated DiCE metrics tables using established templates and validation rules
• Clean, organize, and transform large datasets to ensure accurate metrics reporting
• Perform consistency checks to verify alignment across data sources
• Track progress against defined validation milestones and report status to project leads
• C&E Documentation Validation
• Execute tag-by-tag comparisons between Cause & Effect (C&E) documentation and reference sources (e.g., instrument lists, I/O lists, logic narratives)
• Identify discrepancies including missing tags, naming mismatches, duplicate entries, or incorrect associations
• Log and document findings in structured tracking tools with clear, repeatable evidence
• SIS / Controls Tag Validation
• Validate SIS and control system tags for completeness
